steps.title: Use the intercept shortcut
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the point where the graph crosses the $y$-axis. Every point on the $y$-axis has $x=0$, so the point has the form $(0,y)$.

steps.title: Read the graph
steps.content: On the graph, the curve meets the $y$-axis at the marked point $(0,5)$. Since the problem writes the intercept as $(0,y)$, the requested value is the second coordinate: $y=5$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is $(0,5)$, so the value of $y$ is 5.
